expectations.evidence: Formatted table in '## Trade-off Matrix' section with 8 quality attributes as rows and 3 options as columns, using +/- notation for each cell.
3 options analyzed: (1) Full Microservices Extraction, (2) Modular Monolith with Targeted Scaling, (3) Strangler Fig: Extract Only the Order Processing Hot Path.

Explicitly names 8 quality attributes: Elasticity, Simplicity (Operational), Time-to-market, Cost, Fault isolation, Maintainability, Reliability, Testability. Top 3 driving attributes identified with rationale.

Clear recommendation: 'Option 3: Strangler Fig (Extract Order Hot Path Only)' with primary justification (Elasticity), secondary justification (Operational simplicity + Time-to-market), and detailed reasoning.
Section 'Acknowledged downsides and why they're acceptable' lists 3 explicit negatives for Option 3: data synchronization complexity, two systems to maintain, and temporary architecture.
Recommendation explicitly states: 'Option 3: Strangler Fig (Extract Order Hot Path Only) -- the least worst choice for this context.'
Analysis directly references user constraints: 12 developers, zero microservices experience, 9-month hard deadline (next Black Friday), 30x traffic spikes, 200k LOC Django monolith, 3 years old.
Option 1 (Full Microservices) has 6 disadvantages. Option 2 (Modular Monolith) has 5 disadvantages. Option 3 (Strangler Fig) has 5 disadvantages. All well above the 2-minimum threshold.
